lead_headline: Foreclosure filings surge 145% week-over-week to 49 new cases, led by an $8.1M Arapahoe County filing — while restaurant activity cools to zero openings today after 18 debuts last week.

restaurant.blurb: Zero openings, zero closures today — a rare standstill after last week's 18 openings and 16 closures. That's a sharp reversal from the prior seven days (28 opens, 47 shutters), suggesting the market caught its breath. Recent activity scattered across neighborhoods: Reunion Bread launched in Platt Park, Takiza Taco closed in Union Station, and a Quiznos in Thornton flickered on-and-off in our tracking. The 30-day tally sits at 36 openings against 61 closures — net negative continues.

foreclosure.blurb: 49 new filings this week — a 145% jump from last week's 20 — pushing total active cases to 1,539. The week's filings total $22.2M, headlined by an $8.1M Arapahoe County property on S. Clinton Street. Weld and Larimer counties lead volume (285 and 259 active cases), while 50 auctions loom in the next seven days. The spike suggests distress is accelerating after a quieter February stretch.

retail.blurb: Retail lease and occupancy tracking in development — data pipeline under construction.

revenue.blurb: Sales tax and revenue analysis tool planned for metro business intelligence.

reputation.blurb: Review sentiment and reputation monitoring system on the roadmap.
